titleStmt.title: SII 3.76: original edition by Eugen Julius Theodor Hultzsch
VI.—INSCRIPTIONS OF KULOTTUNGA-CHOLA I. No. 76.—INSCRIPTION IN THE JAMBUKESVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This inscription (No. 31 of 1891) is engraved on the east wall of the second prākāra of the Jambukēśvara templeFor a few other inscriptions in the same temple see Ind. Ant. Vol. XXI. p. 121, and Ep. Ind. Vol. III. pp. 9, 10 and 72. on the island of Śrīraṅgam near Trichinopoly. As stated in Vol. II. p. 253, the ancient name of the locality is Tiruvāṉaikkā, i.e. ‘the sacred elephant-grove,’ and the name of the temple is derived from ‘the sacred white jambū tree’ (tiru-veṇ-ṇāval in Tamil). At the time of the inscription, Tiruvāṉaikkā belonged to Mīgōḻai, a subdivision of the district of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.The city of Tañjāvūr belonged to Tañjāvūr-kūṟṟam, a subdivision of the district of Pāṇḍyakulāśani vaḷanāḍu; above, Vol. II. No. 1, paragraph 2.

The date is the 47th year of the reign of Kulōttuṅga I. A certain Villavarāyaṉ had set up in the temple images of Ṛishabhavāhana, i.e. Śiva riding on the bull, and of Pārvatī. To provide for the requirements of these two images, he purchased from the temple authorities some land, whose crop of paddy had to be made over to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . pukaḻ cūḻnta puṇarilyakaḻ cūḻnta puviyil ponnemiyaḷavuntan nemi naṭappa viḷaṅku cayamakaḷaiyiḷaṅkopparuvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramattoḻilāl putumaṇam puṇarntu matuavaraiyīṭṭam vayirākarattu vāri Ayir
muṉairatakacankkontataḷavaracāar tan taḷamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittu toḷ vali kāṭṭipporppari naṭāttikkīrttiyai niṟutti vaṭaticai vākai cūṭittenticaittemarukamalappūmakaḷ potumaiyum ponniyāṭai naṉnilappāvaiyuniṉtaṉimaiyuntavirap
puṉitattirumaṇimakuṭam Urimaiyiṟcūṭittannaṭiyiraṇṭuntaṭamuṭiyākattonnilaventar cūṭa munṉai maṉuvāṟu peruka kaliyāṟu vaṟuppa ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñcella veṇkuṭaiyirunilavivaḷākam veEṇkaṇuntaṉatu tiruniḻal viṇṇilāttikaḻa
Orutani meruvil puli viḷaiyāṭa vārkaṭaṟṟiīvāntarattuppūpālar tiṟai viṭutanta kalañceori kaḷiṟu muṟai niṟpa vilaṅkiya tenṉavan karuntalai paruntalaittiṭattan ponṉakarppuṟattiṭaikkiṭappavināḷ piṟkulappiṟai pol niṟpiḻai Enṉuñcolletir koṭiṟṟal
latu tan kai villatu koṭā veṟkulattaracar Akḷattiyiṟpaliṭṭa kaḷantaniliṟṟiṉatīṭṭamum paṭṭamum vempariyum viṭṭa tan mānamum kūṟina vīramum kiṭappa Eṟiṉa malaikaḷumutuku neḷippa yiḻinta natikaḷuñcūuḻaṉṟuṭaintoṭa viḻun=ta kaṭalkaḷuntalaivirittalamarakkuṭaticaittannāḷukan=tu tāṉum tānai
nayum pannāḷiṭṭa palapala mutukum payantetir māṟiṉa jayapperuntiruvum paḻiyukan=tu kuṭutta pukaḻin celviyum vāḷāroṇkaṇ maṭan=taaiyariīṭṭamum miīḷātu kuṭutta veṅkari niraiyum kaṅkamaṇṭalamum ciṅkaṇamennum pāṇiyiraṇṭum Oruvicaikkaikkoṇ
ṭiīṇṭiya pukaḻoṭu pāṇṭimaṇṭalaṅkoḷḷattiruviuḷattaṭaittu veḷḷavaruparittaraṅkaḷmum poruparittkkaḷaṅkaḷum polattan=tiravāriyumuṭaittāy van=tu vaṭakaṭal tenkaṭal paṭarvatu polattan peeruñceṉaiyaiyevippañcavar Aivarum poruta porkkaḷat
tañci veru neḷittoṭi Araṇenappukka kāṭaṟattuṭaittu nāṭṭaṭippaṭuttu maṟṟavartammai vaṉacarar tiriyum poccai veñcurameṟṟi koṟṟavijayastampan=ticaitoṟuniṟutti muttin calāpamum muttamiṭpotiyilumattaveṅkari paṭumay
yaccayyamuṅkanniyuṅkaikkoṇṭu punitattennāṭṭellai kāṭṭikkuṭamalaināṭṭuḷḷa cāveṟellāntani vicumpeṟa māveṟiya tan varuūtainiṉittalaivaraikkuṟukalar kulaiyakkoṭṭāṟuṭpaṭa neṟitoṟum nilaikaḷiṭṭaruḷittiṟal koḷ viī
rasiṁhāsaṉantiriya viṭṭaruḷippoṅkoḷipāramun=tiruppuyattalaṅkalum pol viīramuntiyākamum viḷaṅkappār micaitoḻaccivaniṭattumaiyenattiyākavalli Ulakuṭaiyāḷiruppa Avaḷuṭan kaṅkai viīṟṟirun=tena maṅkaai
yar tilatam Eḻicaivallapi Eḻulakamuṭaiyāḷ vāḻi malarntinitiruppa Ūḻiyun=tirumālākattuppiriyātenṟu tirumakaḷirunteṉa viīrasiṁhāsaṉattu Ulakumuṭaiyāḷoṭum viīṟṟirun=taruḷiya kovirājakesaripatmarāna tiripuvanaccakkara
vattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻadevarkku yāṇṭu 4 10 7 Āvatu pāṇṭikulācanivaḷanāṭṭu miīkoḻai devatānabrahmateyam tiruvāṉaikkāvil tiruveṇṇāval kiīḻinitamarntaruḷiya tribhuvaṉapatikku muūlabhṛttyanākiya śaca
ṇḍeśvaran Ādeśam jayaciṅkakulakālavaḷanāṭṭu miīceṅkiḷināṭṭu vaḷampakuṭi Arayamakan muṉaiyan Arumoḻitevanāṉa villavarāyaṉukku nām viṟṟukkuṭutta nilamāvatu . Uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya Emperumān te
vatānam tenkarai Ūrkaḷil pāṇṭikulācanivaḷanāṭṭu mīkoḻai Āḷikuṭiyil Ivaṉukku viṟṟukkuṭutta nilattukkicain=ta kiīḻpārkkellai piḷḷaikoḷḷivāykkālukku meṟkum tenpārkkellai kaḷattil vaṭakkilakaiyvā
ykkālukku vaṭakkum mīpārkkellai Uttamaciīliccaruppetimaṅkalattu tenpiṭākai putukkuṭi Ellaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapārkkellai tennāṟṟaṅkaraipperuvaḻikku teṟku Āka Ivvicain=ta perunānkellaiyuḷpaṭṭa nilam
4 3/4 . Innilam nāle mukkālum Ittevarkku yāṇṭu nāṟpatteḻāvatu varai payirili punceyyāykkuṭṭamumaṇṇumiṭṭukkiṭan=tamaiyilannilam muṉaiyan Arumoḻitevanāṉa villavarāyaṉukku viṟṟukkuṭuttu
kkoḷvatāka Emmilicain=ta vilaipporuḷ Anṟāṭu naṟkācu 4 1/20 1/80 . Ikkācu nāle mākāṇiyuṅkaikkoṇṭu tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya Emperumān śrīpaṇṭārattu Oṭukki muṉaiyan Arumoḻitevaṉāna villavarāyaṉukku viṟṟukkuṭut
tu Ivan Uṭaiyār tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya Emperumān koyilil Iṭaṅkaināyakarenṟu Eḻun=taruḷuvitta Iṣabhavāhaṉadevarkkum nampirāṭṭiyārkku tirumañcanaṅkaḷukku tiruvamirtupaṭikku Iraṇṭu tirunāḷilum Iraṇṭu nāḷ tiruviḻā Eḻun=taruḷukaikku Uḷḷiṭṭu
veṇṭum niman=taṅkaḷukku Innila 4 3/4 kallittirutti śrībhaṇṭārattu puncey varicaiyāl veli Onṟukku nel Aiṅkalamāka rājakesarimarakkālāl Innilam nāle mukkālukkum Aḷappatāna 2 10 3 kaḷa vata ṅa . Innel Irupattumukkalane Irutūṇikkuṟu
ṇiyum Aḷakkumaiṭattil kār pāti pacānam pāti Aḷappatākavum . Itil mikiti koṇṭu Ivan Iṭaṅkaināyakarenṟu Eḻun=taruḷuvitta Iṣabhavāhanadevarkkum nampirāṭṭiyārkkum tirumañcaṉapaṭikaḷukkum tiruvamutupaṭikkum Iraṇṭu tirunāḷilum Iraṇṭu nāḷeḻuḷan=taruḷukaikku
Uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭunimantaṅkaḷukku nimantañceytu kuṭuttom . muṉaiyan Arumoḻitevanāṉa villavarāyanukku tiruvāṉaikkāvuṭaiya Emperumānukku muūlabhṛttyanākiya ścaṇḍeśvaran Uḷḷiṭṭa kanmikaḷom . Itu panmāheśvararakṣai .

app.note: The ka of nāyaka had been originally omitted and was subsequently entered.
This [[ja]] is the usual abbrevation for nel, ‘paddy;’ the following symbols represent the quantities and measures which are repeated in words in the next sentence.
This seems to be corrected by the engraver from mikuti which is, however, more correct.

div.p: (Line 11.) In the 47th year (of the reign) of king Rājakēsarivarman, alias the emperor of the three worlds, Śrī-Kulōttuṅga-Śōḻadēva, who etc.The historical introduction of this inscription is the same as in No. 73. —(at) the order of Chaṇḍēśvara,See above, p. 167, note 9. who is the chief servant of the lord of the three worlds who is pleased to reside under the sacred white jambū) tree (tiru-veṇ-ṇāval) at Tiruvāṉaikkā, a dēvadāna (and) brahmadēya in Mīgōḻai, (a subdivision) of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu, we sold the following land to Muṉaiyaṉ Arumoḻidēvaṉ, alias Villavarāyaṉ, the son of Arayaṉ of Vaḷambaguḍi in Mīśeṅgiḷi-nāḍu, (a subdivision) of Jayasiṁhakulakālavaḷanāḍu.According to Vol. II. No. 66, paragraphs 469 and 474, Miśeṅgiḷi-nāḍu was a subdivision of the district of Pāṇḍyakulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.

(L. 13.) The eastern boundary agreed on of the land which (we) sold to him at Āḷiguḍi in Mīgōḻai, (a subdivision) of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu, (one) among the villages on the southern bank (of the Kāvērī) (and) a dēvadāna of the lord Emberumāṉ of Tiruvāṉaikkā, (is) to the west of the Piḷḷaigoḷḷi) channel; the southern boundary (is) to the north of the Agai channel on the north of the thrashing-floor; the western boundary (is) to the east of the boundary of Pudukkuḍi, a hamlet on the south of Uttamaśīlichaturvēdimaṅgalam;A village of the same name is mentioned in Vol. II. No. 57, paragrap �. (and) the northern boundary (is) to the south of the high road on the southern bank of the river. Altogether, 4 3/4 (vēli) of land, enclosed within these four great boundaries agreed on.

(L. 16.) As these four and three quarters (vēli) of land had been lying full of holes and sand as uncultivated dry land until the forty-seventh year (of the reign) of this king, we agreed to sell that land to Muṉaiyaṉ Arumoḻidēvaṉ, alias Villavarāyaṉ, for a purchase-money of 4, 1/20, 1/80 good kāśu current at the time.

(L. 17.) Having received these four, one twentieth and one eightieth kāśu and having deposited (them) in the treasury of the temple of Emberumāṉ of Tiruvāṉaikkā, (we) sold (the land) to Muṉaiyaṉ Arumoḻidēvaṉ, alias) Villavarāyaṉ.

(L. 18.) Having dug and reclaimed these 4 3/4 (vēli) of land, (he) has to supply for these four and three quarters (vēli) of land to the temple treasury 23 kalam, 2 tūṇi and 1 kuṟuṇi of paddy by the marakkāl (called after) Rājakēsarin,On this measure see above, Vol. II. p. 42. (viz.) five kalam for each vēli at the rate for dry land, for the expenses required by the god Ṛishabhavāhana—whom he had set up under the name IḍaṅgaināyagarI.e. ‘the lord on the left hand (of Pārvatī).’ in the temple of the lord Emberumāṉ of Tiruvāṉaikkā—and by (his) consort, (viz). for bathing the idols, for oblations, for carrying them about on two days at (each of) the two festivals, etc.
(L. 19.) If those twenty-three kalam, two tūṇi and one kuṟuṇi of paddy are supplied, half has to be supplied in kār (and) half in paśāṉam.Kār and paśāṉam are two different kinds of paddy.

(L. 20.) Having received this in full, we made provision for the expenses required by the god Ṛishabhavāhana whom he had set up under the name Iḍaṅgaināyagar, and by (his) consort, (viz). for bathing the idols, for oblations, for carrying them about on two days at (cach of) the two festivals, etc.

(L. 21.) (This is an agreement made by) us, the Pūjāris, including Chaṇḍēśvara, who is the chief servant of Emberumāṉ of Tiruvāṉaikkā, with Muṉaiyaṉ Arumoḻidēvaṉ, alias Villavarāyaṉ. This (is placed under) the protection of all Māhēśvaras.
Digital edition of SII 3.76 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
